steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find the side length of the equilateral triangle
steps.content:
Perimeter = 912 cm. For an equilateral triangle, all three sides are equal:
$$s = \frac{912}{3} = 304 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Use the equilateral circumradius shortcut
steps.content:
For an equilateral triangle with side length $s$, the radius of the circle through its vertices is
$$R=\frac{s}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{3}$$
With $s=304$,
$$R=\frac{304\sqrt{3}}{3}$$

steps.title: Solve for w using the given form
steps.content:
The problem says the radius is $w\sqrt{3}$ centimeters, so set
$$w\sqrt{3}=\frac{304\sqrt{3}}{3}$$
Divide by $\sqrt{3}$:
$$w=\frac{304}{3}$$
Accepted entries include 304/3 or 101.33.
